REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT-ADJUDICATION (TRANSACTION ID # 54929804) FILED BY DEFENDANT FEDEX GROUND PACKAGE SYSTEM, INC., INCORRECTLY SUED AS FED EX GROUND PACKAGING SYSTEM, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT FEDEX GROUND PACKAGE SYSTEM, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFFS CANNOT ESTABLISH THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT/GUARANTEE. THE CONTRACT IS TOO UNCERTAIN AND THE STATUTE OF FRAUDS BARS THE CLAIM. PLAINTIFF RAFIK EL-HANI'S DECLARATION, WHICH STATES THAT DEFENDANT GUARANTEED $250,000, CONTRADICTS HIS DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND DOES NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT HAD A PERSONAL, IMMEDIATE, AND PECUNIARY INTEREST IN THE TRANSACTION AND THEREFORE, THE EXCEPTION TO THE STATUTE OF FRAUDS DOES NOT APPLY. PLAINTIFFS CANNOT ESTABLISH THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE UCL BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS HAD AN ADEQUATE REMEDY AT LAW, PLAINTIFFS CANNOT OBTAIN AN INJUNCTION BECAUSE IT IS UNLIKELY THAT THE ALLEGED PRACTICE WILL OCCUR AGAIN, AND DEFENDANT HAS NOTHING TO RESTORE TO PLAINTIFFS. PLAINTIFFS CANNOT ESTABLISH THE SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR CONCEALMENT AND FRAUD BECAUSE THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF SCIENTER AND PLAINTIFFS WERE AWARE THAT THE TERMINATION PACKAGE NEEDED TO BE FORWARDED TO CORPORATE HEADQUARTERS. PLAINTIFFS CANNOT ESTABLISH THE EXTORTION CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE DEFENDANT ACTED WITHIN ITS CONTRACTUAL RIGHTS, I.E., THERE WAS NO DURESS, AND DEFENDANT NEVER RECEIVED ANY PROPERTY FROM PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH THE UNRUH ACT CLAIM BECAUSE IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT THE DECISION MAKERS DID NOT EXPRESS ANY DISCRIMINATORY ANIMUS. THE COURT ALSO RULED ON THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively, Summ Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-07-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARCUS DANIEL MERCHASIN, TEL. NO. 678-2700 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. STEVEN F. HELFAND OF THE HELFAND LAW OFFICES, TEL. NO. 397-0007 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. MELINDA S. RIECHERT OF THE MORGAN LEWIS, TEL. NO. (650) 843-7530 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FEDEX GROUND PACKAGE SYSTEM, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication -
The Court grants the motion for summary judgment. Plaintiffs cannot establish the third cause of action for breach of contract/guarantee. The contract is too uncertain and the statute of frauds bars the claim. Plaintiff Rafik El-Hani's declaration, which states that defendant guaranteed $250,000, contradicts his deposition testimony and does not create a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiffs failed to show that defendant had a personal, immediate, and pecuniary interest in the transaction and therefore, the exception to the statute of frauds does not apply. Plaintiffs cannot establish the fourth cause of action for violation of the UCL because plaintiffs had an adequate remedy at law, plaintiffs cannot obtain an injunction because it is unlikely that the alleged practice will occur again, and defendant has nothing to restore to plaintiffs. Plaintiffs cannot establish the sixth and seventh causes of action for concealment and fraud because there is no evidence of scienter and plaintiffs were aware that the termination package needed to be forwarded to corporate headquarters. Plaintiffs cannot establish the extortion cause of action because defendant acted within its contractual rights, i.e., there was no duress, and defendant never received any property from plaintiff. Plaintiff cannot establish the Unruh Act claim because it is undisputed that the decision makers did not express any discriminatory animus. THE COURT ALSO RULED ON THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

Ntc Of Mtn To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-25-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS RAFIK EL-HANI AND EL-HANI SERVICES INC.'S MOTION TO AMEND COMPLAINT.

Appearances: Marcus Daniel Merchasin (415.678.2700) and Steven F. Helfand of the Helfand Law Offices (415.397.0007) for plaintiffs and moving parties; Melinda S. Riechert of Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP (650.843.7530) appearing via Court Call for defendant and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiffs' motion for leave to amend is granted. Plaintiffs have made the requisite showing that there was no undue delay in bringing the present motion, Defendant will not be prejudiced by the amendment, and that justice will be served by the amendment. (See CCP 473). Court and counsel accept Plaintiffs stipulation that defendant's summary judgment motion may be filed 60 days before trial, and plaintiffs are directed to submit a form of order that includes this language. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR #6185 (302/EHG)
